feature_name: Dual operation modes: Root Complex and End Point
description: The PCIe core supports dual operation as Root Complex (RC) or End Point (EP). Mode is selected by bootstrap pins PCIESSMODE[1:0] and can be overridden in software. In EP, the device operates as a legacy or native PCIe endpoint; in RC it enumerates downstream devices. BAR0 in both modes maps the application register space. Software must configure mode before link training.
dependencies: Set PCIESSMODE, device-level DEVSTAT; ensure LTSSM_EN sequencing
Remarks: Choose mode early and avoid changing after link-up; follow respective initialization sequences for RC or EP.

feature_name: PCIe link widths ×1/×2 with dynamic width conversion
description: The module implements a single bidirectional link that can be configured as ×1 or ×2 lanes. It supports dynamic-width conversion allowing operation across lane counts. Automatic lane reversal is supported to handle swapped routing. Polarity inversion on receive is available per lane. Lane enable is managed via PL_LINK_CTRL and PL_GEN2 lane fields.
dependencies: Proper SerDes configuration; LINK training; board routing considerations
Remarks: Validate negotiated link width via LINK_STAT_CTRL[NEGOTIATED_LINK_WD] and debug registers after training.

feature_name: Gen1/Gen2 link speeds (2.5 and 5.0 GT/s) with directed speed change
description: The PHY and core support 2.5 GT/s (Gen1) and 5.0 GT/s (Gen2) operation. The PLL output is 2.5 GHz in both modes with internal bus width conversion at Gen2. Directed speed change to Gen2 is triggered by PL_GEN2[DIR_SPD]. Link control 2 exposes target speed and compliance pattern controls.
dependencies: PLL lock; stable reference clock; proper de-emphasis/swing settings
Remarks: After Gen1 link-up, set DIR_SPD and monitor LINK_STAT_CTRL[LINK_SPEED] to confirm Gen2.

feature_name: Automatic lane reversal and polarity inversion
description: The receiver can automatically handle lane reversal conditions during training. Per-lane polarity inversion controls exist on both TX and RX paths. These features ease PCB routing and interoperability. Controls are provided in SERDES_CFGx for polarity and loopback options. Debug registers indicate lane reversal detection status.
dependencies: Correct SerDes configuration; verify LTSSM training states
Remarks: Use DEBUG1 fields during bring-up to confirm lane reversal handling on problematic links.

feature_name: Single Virtual Channel and Traffic Class
description: The controller implements a single virtual channel (VC) and a single traffic class (TC). This simplifies arbitration and is adequate for many embedded use cases. It also filters posted, non-posted, and completion traffic. Multiple VCs and TCs are not supported.
dependencies: System QoS expectations must align with single VC/TC
Remarks: Plan system traffic accordingly since VC/TC expansion is not available.

feature_name: Payload and request size limits
description: Maximum outbound payload size is 128 bytes and maximum inbound payload is 256 bytes. The maximum remote read request size is 256 bytes. These sizes impact DMA descriptors and transaction batching. Configure PCIe capabilities to advertise supported sizes.
dependencies: Endpoint/host capability negotiation
Remarks: Size mismatches can degrade performance; verify negotiated sizes in configuration space.

feature_name: VBUSM master and slave ports (128-bit) to SoC interconnect
description: The module exposes a 128-bit VBUSM master port for inbound TLPs and a 128-bit VBUSM slave port for outbound transfers and MMR access. These ports bridge PCIe traffic to internal memory and register spaces. The master issues SoC reads/writes on behalf of PCIe initiators. The slave accepts local initiator accesses destined for PCIe.
dependencies: SoC interconnect bandwidth; arbitration and priority settings
Remarks: Tune PRIORITY[MST_PRIORITY,PRIVID] for inbound QoS and ensure cacheability attributes are non-cacheable for MMR spaces.

feature_name: Address Space 0 mapping (Application, Local Config, Remote Config, Remote IO)
description: A 16 KB Address Space 0 contains four 4 KB regions: application registers, local configuration space, remote configuration access window, and remote IO access window (RC only). These regions are non-burst, 32-bit only, and must be non-cacheable. Remote accesses require a live link and software guard checks.
dependencies: Link operational; proper CFG_SETUP and IOBASE programming
Remarks: Avoid remote window accesses when link is down; implement checks and timeouts to prevent bus hangs.

feature_name: Address Space 1 mapping for data transfer
description: Address Space 1 is dedicated to data movement between the SoC and PCIe peers. BAR values define where endpoints are mapped in the host memory map. In RC mode, regions outside downstream Base/Limit windows are forwarded upstream. The PCIe subsystem translates transactions accordingly.
dependencies: RC Base/Limit windows and endpoint BAR programming
Remarks: Ensure Base/Limit registers reflect the full downstream map to prevent misrouting and unintended RC captures.

feature_name: Outbound address translation (32 regions, 1–8 MB each)
description: Outbound TLPs use a 32-region translator with sizes of 1, 2, 4, or 8 MB set by OB_SIZE. Each region remaps internal addresses to PCIe space using OB_OFFSET_INDEXn/OB_OFFSETn_HI. Translation applies to memory transactions and must not cross region boundaries. Software configures regions prior to enabling traffic.
dependencies: Set CMD_STATUS[OB_XLT_EN]; size-index alignment per OB_SIZE
Remarks: Choose region sizes to minimize boundary crossings; large bursts must not span regions.

feature_name: Inbound address translation (4 regions) with BAR association
description: Inbound memory requests are remapped to internal addresses via four regions keyed by BAR matches. IB_BARn selects the BAR, and IB_STARTn_LO/HI with IB_OFFSETn define the mapping. BAR0 is reserved for application registers and cannot be remapped. In 64-bit addressing, BAR pairs are concatenated as documented.
dependencies: Set CMD_STATUS[IB_XLT_EN]; correct BAR programming in config space
Remarks: Plan BAR layout so data windows map to appropriate internal SRAM/DDR regions without overlap.

feature_name: BAR Mask registers for EP pre-configuration via DBI_CS2
description: BAR Mask registers overlay the BARs to program requested sizes and attributes before RC enumeration. Software enables DBI_CS2 (CMD_STATUS[5]) to write mask values then clears it. BAR mask bits are write-only; verify by writing test patterns to BARs. BAR0 mask is limited as BAR0 maps application registers.
dependencies: Enable DBI_CS2; perform readback validation; disable DBI_CS2 before enumeration
Remarks: Never modify BAR masks from the serial link side; perform local writes only to avoid undefined behavior.

feature_name: Endian mode conversion for inbound and outbound transactions
description: The PCIESS supports little and big endian modes with programmable byte-swapping on 1-, 2-, 4-, or 8-byte boundaries. ENDIAN register selects the swizzle mode in big-endian systems. Inbound and outbound conversion tables define how data and byte enables are reordered. MMR and non-prefetchable operations use 32-bit boundaries.
dependencies: System endianness selection; consistent DMA descriptors and software structures
Remarks: Validate data integrity with test patterns across all sizes and alignments after configuring endian mode.

feature_name: SerDes PLL and reference clock configuration with lock status
description: SerDes PLL is configured via PCIE_SERDES_CFGPLL with MPY and bandwidth settings based on ref clock. LOCK status is read from PCIE_SERDES_STS to gate accesses. The PHY multiplies ref clock to 2.5 GHz for both Gen1 and Gen2 bus operations. Proper jitter and common clock practices are required for reliable links.
dependencies: Device-level kicker unlock; stable REFCLK; wait 1 µs then poll LOCK
Remarks: Do not access PCIESS registers before PLL lock; configure loop bandwidth per clock quality to optimize jitter tolerance.

feature_name: Gen2 de-emphasis and TX swing controls
description: For Gen2 operation the controller exposes de-emphasis level selection and TX swing configuration. PL_GEN2[DEEMPH,CFG_TX_SWING] and LINK_CTRL2[SEL_DEEMPH,DE_EMPH] manage electrical characteristics. These settings tailor transmitter behavior to channel loss. Polling-compliance and modified compliance controls aid validation.
dependencies: Link at 5.0 GT/s; board channel characteristics
Remarks: Coordinate settings with SI analysis; verify with compliance tests and adjust NUM_FTS and margins as needed.

feature_name: Clock control and common clock architecture support
description: The link may use a common reference clock between partners as required for SSC systems. Software sets COMMON_CLK_CFG in LINK_STAT_CTRL when a common clock is not used. Link training sequence counts are increased without a shared clock. The PLL and clocks must be stable prior to LTSSM enable.
dependencies: Stable differential REFCLK; SSC system considerations
Remarks: Ensure both ends agree on common clock usage to prevent extended training and interoperability issues.

feature_name: Reset mechanisms: hot, warm, and power domain transitions
description: The PCIESS supports software-initiated hot reset via RSTCMD[INIT_RST], warm reset, and device-level power-on reset. After reset, software must wait at least 100 ms before issuing transactions. Power domain off/on resets the module and requires re-initialization and link training. Sticky bits in MMRs persist across soft reset.
dependencies: PSC power control sequence; EOI and traffic quiesce; FLUSH_N status
Remarks: Stop all in-flight traffic before power cycling; re-run the documented initialization sequence for RC or EP.

feature_name: PIPE loopback (master/slave) and PHY loopback for test
description: The PCIESS supports PIPE loopback in both master and slave roles for symbol-level validation. PHY loopback can be enabled (KeyStone I) to loop TX to RX without a partner, enabling TLP loopback in RC mode with specific steps. Loopback requires proper SerDes and PL registers and should be set before training to avoid sequence mismatches.
dependencies: RC mode for PHY loopback; FORCE_LINK to POLL_ACTIVE; BAR/limits considerations
Remarks: Avoid configuration transactions during PHY loopback; ensure BAR windows do not capture local writes inadvertently.

feature_name: Interrupt architecture with 14 SoC interrupt lines
description: The PCIESS maps multiple events into 14 SoC interrupt lines, including four legacy INTx (RC), MSI vectors, error reporting, and power/reset events. Each has raw, enable, and status registers. MSI events are grouped in sets of four per line. EOI handling is via IRQ_EOI with event number semantics.
evidence: Section 2.14.1; Table 2-10; MSIn_* and LEGACY_* (3.1.xx); IRQ_EOI (3.1.15)
dependencies: SoC interrupt controller configuration; per-source enable/clear
Remarks: Document IRQ routing and service ordering; clear status then write EOI with the correct event index to prevent retrigger.

feature_name: Legacy interrupt generation in EP mode
description: An EP can generate a legacy interrupt using EP_IRQ_SET/CLR to assert and deassert INTA/B/C/D messages. The interrupt type used is configured in the INT_PIN register. Only one legacy interrupt can be pending until a deassert is sent. Status is visible in EP_IRQ_STATUS.
dependencies: Enable LEGACY_X_IRQ in LEGACY_*_IRQ_ENABLE_SET; correct INT_PIN value
Remarks: Issue deassert promptly after handling to allow subsequent interrupts; consider MSI for higher scale.

feature_name: MSI generation from EP and MSI reception in RC
description: MSI is generated by performing a PCIe 32-bit (or 64-bit) memory write to the configured MSI address with MSI data. The controller exposes MSI address and data registers and an MSI_IRQ register at RC BAR0 for vector writes. RC receives MSI interrupts via MSIn_IRQ_STATUS and requires status clear and EOI writes before re-arming.
dependencies: MSI_CAP[MSI_EN]; disable legacy when MSI enabled; vector mapping
Remarks: Ensure relaxed ordering hazards are considered; sequence data writes before MSI write if ordering is required.

feature_name: MSI reception in EP (non-standard support)
description: Although standard EPs typically do not receive MSI, the PCIESS supports MSI reception via MSIn_IRQ_* registers. MSI arrivals set status bits which must be cleared with write-1-to-clear and EOI signaling. This enables EP-to-EP interrupt use cases through BAR0 space. Software must identify the source.
dependencies: RC/EP partners must target EP BAR0 MSI registers; proper vector planning
Remarks: Maintain a software mapping of vectors to sources when multiple peers can generate MSI to the EP.

feature_name: Power management: device D-states and ASPM L0s/L1
description: The module supports PCI device D0/D1/D2/D3hot/cold states and ASPM L0s and L1 link states. Device and link states have defined relationships, and software can program entry policies. PMCMD/PMCFG and PM capability/control registers manage messages and power state requests. L2 state is not supported.
dependencies: System power architecture; COMMON_CLK and ACK timers; partner support
Remarks: Use ASPM judiciously to balance latency and power; verify exit latencies and indicators before enabling in production.

feature_name: Baseline error reporting (PCI-compatible and PCIe device control)
description: Baseline error reporting is controlled via STATUS_COMMAND and DEV_STAT_CTRL. Software enables SERR and parity response and can check status for parity, aborts, and unsupported requests. Device Status/Control enables generation of correctable, non-fatal, fatal, and unsupported request messages. Status bits are write-1-to-clear.
dependencies: Enable *_RP bits as needed; ensure host handles messages
Remarks: Set only the reporting bits your system services; otherwise spurious messages may escalate to SERR at the host.

feature_name: Advanced Error Reporting (AER) with masks, severity, and logs
description: AER registers expose uncorrectable and correctable status with mask and severity controls. Software can choose which errors generate messages and whether they are treated as fatal or non-fatal. Header log registers capture the offending TLP header. Root-side registers collect error counts and the source IDs.
dependencies: Enable ROOT_ERR_CMD bits and appropriate masks; handle ECRC settings coherently
Remarks: Tune masks/severity to application criticality; avoid enabling ECRC if peers do not support it (see usage note).

feature_name: ECRC generation/check control
description: End-to-end CRC support is programmable, enabling generation on outgoing TLPs and checking on receipts. Controls reside in PCIE_ACCR[ECRC_GEN_EN,ECRC_CHK_EN]. If peers do not implement ECRC, these should be disabled to avoid false errors. First Error Pointer aids triage when multiple errors occur quickly.
dependencies: Peer capability discovery; coordinated configuration across link
Remarks: If RC enables ECRC, ensure all endpoints agree; misconfiguration can flood the bus with AER reports.

feature_name: Diagnostic error injection (LCRC/ECRC invert) and PCS error features
description: Diagnostic controls allow forcing an LCRC or ECRC error on the next packet for validation. DIAG_CTRL bits self-clear after injection. PCS_CFGx registers also provide error injection controls for SerDes/PCS testing. These aids support compliance and robustness testing without external equipment.
dependencies: Do not use in production; isolate test paths; ensure monitors are enabled
Remarks: Use along with AER handlers to verify software response to link-layer and transaction-layer faults.

feature_name: ACK frequency and replay/latency timers control
description: The Ack Frequency register can throttle ACK DLLPs by acknowledging every N TLPs, optimizing bandwidth. PL_ACKTIMER and SYM_NUM provide programmable replay and ACK/Nak latency timer modifiers. These parameters influence retry behavior and throughput under error or congestion. Defaults are provided and can be tuned.
dependencies: Stable link operation; align with partner tolerances; compliance limits
Remarks: Excessive ACK spacing can degrade fairness; adjust conservatively and validate under stress and error conditions.

feature_name: Transaction requirements: bus mastering, alignment, bursts, and byte strobes
description: Transactions must obey several constraints on the internal bus: enable bus mastering before initiating upstream requests, limit maximum transfer size to 128 bytes, and use incremental bursts only. Misaligned outbound writes above 120 bytes and byte strobe holes are not allowed. Read responses may be split but not interleaved beyond max configured size.
dependencies: Set STATUS_COMMAND[BUS_MS]; respect 128-byte boundary rules
Remarks: Enforce these constraints in DMA drivers; add assertions on strobe patterns and alignments to avoid lockups.

feature_name: External DMA support via SoC EDMA (no internal DMA)
description: The PCIESS has no integrated DMA but interoperates with SoC EDMA for outbound/inbound moves. Examples are provided for memory write and read using 128-byte payloads. Bandwidth expectations are ~85% of link layer throughput per lane and direction under ideal conditions. Inbound transfers arrive via the master port.
dependencies: Proper outbound translation and BAR mapping; EDMA configuration
Remarks: Size EDMA aCnt/bCnt to payload size and buffer length; ensure cache coherence and doorbell sequencing.

feature_name: Privileged access and transaction priority control
description: PRIORITY[MST_PRIV] enforces supervisor mode for PCIe master transactions targeting device registers. MST_PRIORITY sets inbound transaction QoS on the master port, and PRIVID tags the privilege domain. These controls help protect registers and arbitrate bus access. Defaults are programmable via application registers.
dependencies: SoC privilege model; inbound master uses correct PRIVID
Remarks: Audit access paths to application registers, ensuring only privileged initiators can reach them via BAR0.

feature_name: Outbound IO access support in RC mode only
description: The controller supports outbound IO cycles from RC via a 4 KB internal window, with IOBASE providing the TLP base. IO access is not supported inbound in RC or EP mode. Maximum IO transfer is a single 32-bit aligned access; bursts are not permitted.
dependencies: RC mode; IOBASE configuration; 32-bit alignment
Remarks: Prefer memory-mapped access; use IO only when required by legacy devices and keep operations minimal.

feature_name: Message support and traffic filtering with configurable masks
description: The module supports PCIe message TLPs for transmit and receive and provides filtering for posted, non-posted, and completions. Filter mask registers can relax or enforce matching (e.g., tag, function, attribute) and control vendor messages and unexpected completions. These controls aid debug and compatibility tuning.
dependencies: Understand partner behavior; avoid masking in production except for known errata
Remarks: Keep restrictive defaults for robustness; selectively open filters only with documented justification.

feature_name: LTSSM state visibility with DEBUG registers and appendix mapping
description: DEBUG0/DEBUG1 expose link training and status signals including LTSSM state, compliance, TS1/TS2, and electrical idle. Appendix A provides a code-to-state map for LTSSM values. These registers help diagnose training failures and lane issues. Fields are read-only and update with link activity.
dependencies: Clocked access after PLL lock; stable link required for meaningful states
Remarks: Capture snapshots during problem windows and correlate with LTSSM codes to pinpoint failing phases.

feature_name: Link control features: common clock, retrain, read completion boundary, compliance entry
description: LINK_STAT_CTRL exposes controls and status including COMMON_CLK_CFG, RETRAIN_LINK (RC only), RCB, and link speed/width. LINK_CTRL2 provides target speed, de-emphasis, and compliance pattern settings. These registers orchestrate training, compliance testing, and compatibility. Some fields are not applicable in EP mode.
dependencies: Mode-dependent applicability; ensure correct bit usage per RC/EP
Remarks: Use retrain sparingly; when altering RCB ensure all drivers agree on boundary to avoid split completion issues.

feature_name: BAR0 inbound mapping to application registers (non-disableable)
description: Inbound TLPs matching BAR0 always target the 4 KB application register space in both RC and EP. This mapping enables control even without EP software and supports MSI mechanisms. There is no provision to disable BAR0 access routing to application registers.
dependencies: Careful exposure of BAR0 in RC memory map; security considerations
Remarks: Restrict BAR0 exposure to trusted masters and ensure privilege checks via PRIORITY where applicable.

feature_name: SerDes lane controls: loopback, equalization, CDR, and polarity
description: SERDES_CFG0/1 provide comprehensive per-lane controls: TX/RX loopback, adaptive equalization, clock/data recovery modes, RX/TX polarity inversion, and loss-of-signal detection. These aid bring-up over challenging channels and lab debug. Settings should match channel characteristics and be validated with link training.
dependencies: PLL lock; correct MPY and ref clock; avoid conflicting settings with LTSSM
Remarks: Start with recommended defaults, then adjust RX_EQ and RX_CDR for SSC or fixed-offset systems as needed.
